source.title: Behavioral correlates of activity in identified hypocretin/orexin neurons

confidence_rationale: Identified-cell single-unit recordings in freely-moving rats covering the full sleep-wake-active behaviour cycle. Establishes the ~5x active/quiet recruitment ratio and the episode-scale firing kinetics that anchor MECH-280's sustained_threat_window and recruitment_threshold parameters. High-quality primary evidence with cell-identification controls.
failure_signatures: If orexin neurons fired episodically rather than tonically across behavioural states, MECH-280's sustained_threat_window EMA-style integration would be the wrong substrate; the recordings show sustained elevation across whole episodes of active waking, supporting an integration-window model.

Sensory-evoked transient activation is sub-second; if MECH-280's override_signal kinetics matched these transients, override authority would dissipate too quickly to produce sustained foraging-under-threat. The episode-scale carry-over the recordings document is what justifies the slow EMA decay (decay_rate = 0.05).

mapping.source_claim_statement: Identified hypocretin/orexin neurons fire transiently to single sensory stimuli (sub-second to seconds) but show sustained elevation across whole episodes of active waking lasting tens of seconds to minutes; firing is moderate during grooming and eating, maximal during exploration.
mapping.ree_translation: The episode-scale firing kinetics directly anchor MECH-280's sustained_threat_window (12 steps; biologically defensible band 8-25 steps) and decay_rate (EMA half-life ~14 steps); the active/quiet ratio anchors the recruitment_threshold midpoint.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Recordings are in rat under sleep-wake monitoring with food and grooming opportunities; the threat / metabolic-demand contingencies MECH-280 cares about are not the explicit experimental contrast. The mapping is from active-waking firing more generally to override_signal recruitment under sustained drive plus threat, which is one step removed.
mapping.source_context: Rat; freely moving across sleep-wake cycle; juxtacellular labelling for cell identification; single-unit recordings; behavioural state classification by EEG/EMG plus video.

confidence_components.notes: Neuron-tier single-unit primary paper with cell-identification controls. Mapping fidelity discounted because the experimental contrast is sleep-wake-active rather than threat-foraging arbitration. Transfer risk standard.
